title: Low maintainability score (0.288) — large modules/functions
evidence: scores.json maintainability=0.2883; knowledge_graph.py is 610 lines with a 56-line standings() and data_loader.py 376 lines of per-file parsers
suggestion: Split per-competition CSV loaders and the standings accumulator into smaller helpers to lift maintainability

title: Code-quality scorer below ceiling (0.667)
evidence: scores.json code_quality=0.6667 (ruff-based quality scorer); e.g. compound statements on one line in standings() (knowledge_graph.py:487-491)
suggestion: Address the ruff findings (avoid multi-statement lines) to raise code_quality

title: No-op branch in find_matches
evidence: knowledge_graph.py:203-204 — `if opp_key is not None and team_key is not None and opp_key == team_key: pass` does nothing

title: MCP import is guarded so engine + tests run without the package
evidence: server.py:34-37 wraps `from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP` in try/except; build_server() raises a clear message if absent
suggestion: None — keeps the data/query layer and its tests hermetic

title: Cross-source fixture deduplication by source priority
evidence: knowledge_graph.py:236-263 `_dedup` collapses overlapping fixtures per (competition, season) so standings/aggregates are not double-counted
suggestion: None — beyond-spec correctness handling of overlapping datasets
